title: Word 3 Overview | The 10 words
author: joshualinog
body:
# Word 3 | The 10 words 

## the word
Deuteronomy 5:9-10 HB2 9 5:9 לֹא־תִשְׁתַּחֲוֶה לָהֶם וְלֹא תָעָבְדֵם **כִּי אָנֹכִי יְהוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ אֵל קַנָּא פֹּקֵד עֲון אָבוֹת עַל־בָּנִים עַל־שִׁלֵּשִׁים וְעַל־רִבֵּעִים לְשֹׂנְאָי: 10 5:10 וְעֹשֶׂה חֶסֶד לַאֲלָפִים לְאֹהֲבַי וּלְשֹׁמְרֵי מִצְותָי**:

## translation


## notes
- Word 3 is the heaven version of word eight and they correspond to each other 
- The vocabulary of word three has enormous overlap with the jealous / zealous husband ritual in numbers 5
- The name is the theophanic representative of Yahweh on the Earth
- The name of Yahweh technically is the husband of the people of Israel 
- receiving the name of a man is connected to marriage in the Tanakh
- yahweh's name is revealed to be El-Qana in Word 3 
- yahweh's name is unpacked in Exodus 34 and proclaimed over Moshe, and reveals that El-Qana, El-Burning Obsession, is the kind of overarching the wrapper container of the Yahweh formula. Said differently, his compassion is driven with burning obsession, his graciousness is driven with burning obsession, his slowness to anger is driven with burning obsession, his loyal love is driven with burning obsession, his faithfulness is driven with burning obsession
- because the name of Yahweh, El-Qana is the theophanic representation of Yahweh, he is depicted as an incarnated strongman warrior, circa Exodus 15. This depiction of him is carried through the tanakh, circa Isaiah 59


## Passages 
- Isa 59:16-18 LEB 16 And he saw that there was no man,  and he was appalled that there was no one who intercedes,  so his arm came to assist him,  and his righteousness was what sustained him. 17 And he put on righteousness like a breastplate,  and a helmet of salvation on his head,  and he put on garments of vengeance for clothing,  and he wrapped himself in zeal as in a robe. 18 According to deeds, so he will repay;  wrath to his enemies, requital to those who are his enemies.  He will repay requital to the coastlands.
- Isa 4:1 LEB And seven women shall grasp at one man on that day, saying,  “We will eat our own bread,  and we will wear our own clothing;  only let us be called by your name!  Take away our disgrace!”Isa 4:1 HB2 4:1 וְהֶחֱזִיקוּ שֶׁבַע נָשִׁים בְּאִישׁ אֶחָד בַּיּוֹם הַהוּא לֵאמֹר לַחְמֵנוּ נֹאכֵל וְשִׂמְלָתֵנוּ נִלְבָּשׁ רַק יִקָּרֵא שִׁמְךָ עָלֵינוּ אֱסֹף חֶרְפָּתֵנוּ:
- Exo 34:4-9 LEB 4 And Moses cut two stone tablets like the first ones, and he started early in the morning, and he went up to Mount Sinai, as Yahweh had commanded him, and he took in his hand the two stone tablets. 5 And Yahweh descended in the cloud, and he stood with him there, and he proclaimed the name of Yahweh. 6 And Yahweh passed over before him, and he proclaimed, “Yahweh, Yahweh, God, who is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding with loyal love and faithfulness, 7 keeping loyal love to the thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, and he does not leave utterly unpunished, punishing the guilt of fathers on sons and on sons of sons on third and fourth generations.” 8 And Moses hurried and knelt down to the earth and worshiped. 9 And he said, “Please, if I have found favor in your eyes, Lord, let my Lord, please, go among us—indeed it is a stiff-necked people—and forgive our iniquity and our sin and take us as your possession.”Exo 34:4-9 HB2 4 34:4 וַיִּפְסֹל שְׁנֵי־לֻחֹת אֲבָנִים כָּרִאשֹׁנִים וַיַּשְׁכֵּם מֹשֶׁה בַבֹּקֶר וַיַּעַל אֶל־הַר סִינַי כַּאֲשֶׁר צִוָּה יְהוָה אֹתוֹ וַיִּקַּח בְּיָדוֹ שְׁנֵי לֻחֹת אֲבָנִים: 5 34:5 וַיֵּרֶד יְהוָה בֶּעָנָן וַיִּתְיַצֵּב עִמּוֹ שָׁם וַיִּקְרָא בְשֵׁם יְהוָה: 6 34:6 וַיַּעֲבֹר יְהוָה עַל־פָּנָיו וַיִּקְרָא יְהוָה יְהוָה אֵל רַחוּם וְחַנּוּן אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם וְרַב־חֶסֶד וֶאֱמֶת 7 34:7 נֹצֵר חֶסֶד לָאֲלָפִים נֹשֵׂא עָון וָפֶשַׁע וְחַטָּאָה וְנַקֵּה לֹא יְנַקֶּה פֹּקֵד עֲון אָבוֹת עַל־בָּנִים וְעַל־בְּנֵי בָנִים עַל־שִׁלֵּשִׁים וְעַל־רִבֵּעִים: 8 34:8 וַיְמַהֵר מֹשֶׁה וַיִּקֹּד אַרְצָה וַיִּשְׁתָּחוּ: 9 34:9 וַיֹּאמֶר אִם־נָא מָצָאתִי חֵן בְּעֵינֶיךָ אֲדֹנָי יֵלֶךְ־נָא אֲדֹנָי בְּקִרְבֵּנוּ כִּי עַם־קְשֵׁה־עֹרֶף הוּא וְסָלַחְתָּ לַעֲונֵנוּ וּלְחַטָּאתֵנוּ וּנְחַלְתָּנוּ:
content:
# Word 3 | The 10 words 

## the word
Deuteronomy 5:9-10 HB2 9 5:9 לֹא־תִשְׁתַּחֲוֶה לָהֶם וְלֹא תָעָבְדֵם **כִּי אָנֹכִי יְהוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ אֵל קַנָּא פֹּקֵד עֲון אָבוֹת עַל־בָּנִים עַל־שִׁלֵּשִׁים וְעַל־רִבֵּעִים לְשֹׂנְאָי: 10 5:10 וְעֹשֶׂה חֶסֶד לַאֲלָפִים לְאֹהֲבַי וּלְשֹׁמְרֵי מִצְותָי**:

## translation


## notes
- Word 3 is the heaven version of word eight and they correspond to each other 
- The vocabulary of word three has enormous overlap with the jealous / zealous husband ritual in numbers 5
- The name is the theophanic representative of Yahweh on the Earth
- The name of Yahweh technically is the husband of the people of Israel 
- receiving the name of a man is connected to marriage in the Tanakh
- yahweh's name is revealed to be El-Qana in Word 3 
- yahweh's name is unpacked in Exodus 34 and proclaimed over Moshe, and reveals that El-Qana, El-Burning Obsession, is the kind of overarching the wrapper container of the Yahweh formula. Said differently, his compassion is driven with burning obsession, his graciousness is driven with burning obsession, his slowness to anger is driven with burning obsession, his loyal love is driven with burning obsession, his faithfulness is driven with burning obsession
- because the name of Yahweh, El-Qana is the theophanic representation of Yahweh, he is depicted as an incarnated strongman warrior, circa Exodus 15. This depiction of him is carried through the tanakh, circa Isaiah 59


## Passages 
- Isa 59:16-18 LEB 16 And he saw that there was no man,  and he was appalled that there was no one who intercedes,  so his arm came to assist him,  and his righteousness was what sustained him. 17 And he put on righteousness like a breastplate,  and a helmet of salvation on his head,  and he put on garments of vengeance for clothing,  and he wrapped himself in zeal as in a robe. 18 According to deeds, so he will repay;  wrath to his enemies, requital to those who are his enemies.  He will repay requital to the coastlands.
- Isa 4:1 LEB And seven women shall grasp at one man on that day, saying,  “We will eat our own bread,  and we will wear our own clothing;  only let us be called by your name!  Take away our disgrace!”Isa 4:1 HB2 4:1 וְהֶחֱזִיקוּ שֶׁבַע נָשִׁים בְּאִישׁ אֶחָד בַּיּוֹם הַהוּא לֵאמֹר לַחְמֵנוּ נֹאכֵל וְשִׂמְלָתֵנוּ נִלְבָּשׁ רַק יִקָּרֵא שִׁמְךָ עָלֵינוּ אֱסֹף חֶרְפָּתֵנוּ:
- Exo 34:4-9 LEB 4 And Moses cut two stone tablets like the first ones, and he started early in the morning, and he went up to Mount Sinai, as Yahweh had commanded him, and he took in his hand the two stone tablets. 5 And Yahweh descended in the cloud, and he stood with him there, and he proclaimed the name of Yahweh. 6 And Yahweh passed over before him, and he proclaimed, “Yahweh, Yahweh, God, who is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding with loyal love and faithfulness, 7 keeping loyal love to the thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, and he does not leave utterly unpunished, punishing the guilt of fathers on sons and on sons of sons on third and fourth generations.” 8 And Moses hurried and knelt down to the earth and worshiped. 9 And he said, “Please, if I have found favor in your eyes, Lord, let my Lord, please, go among us—indeed it is a stiff-necked people—and forgive our iniquity and our sin and take us as your possession.”Exo 34:4-9 HB2 4 34:4 וַיִּפְסֹל שְׁנֵי־לֻחֹת אֲבָנִים כָּרִאשֹׁנִים וַיַּשְׁכֵּם מֹשֶׁה בַבֹּקֶר וַיַּעַל אֶל־הַר סִינַי כַּאֲשֶׁר צִוָּה יְהוָה אֹתוֹ וַיִּקַּח בְּיָדוֹ שְׁנֵי לֻחֹת אֲבָנִים: 5 34:5 וַיֵּרֶד יְהוָה בֶּעָנָן וַיִּתְיַצֵּב עִמּוֹ שָׁם וַיִּקְרָא בְשֵׁם יְהוָה: 6 34:6 וַיַּעֲבֹר יְהוָה עַל־פָּנָיו וַיִּקְרָא יְהוָה יְהוָה אֵל רַחוּם וְחַנּוּן אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם וְרַב־חֶסֶד וֶאֱמֶת 7 34:7 נֹצֵר חֶסֶד לָאֲלָפִים נֹשֵׂא עָון וָפֶשַׁע וְחַטָּאָה וְנַקֵּה לֹא יְנַקֶּה פֹּקֵד עֲון אָבוֹת עַל־בָּנִים וְעַל־בְּנֵי בָנִים עַל־שִׁלֵּשִׁים וְעַל־רִבֵּעִים: 8 34:8 וַיְמַהֵר מֹשֶׁה וַיִּקֹּד אַרְצָה וַיִּשְׁתָּחוּ: 9 34:9 וַיֹּאמֶר אִם־נָא מָצָאתִי חֵן בְּעֵינֶיךָ אֲדֹנָי יֵלֶךְ־נָא אֲדֹנָי בְּקִרְבֵּנוּ כִּי עַם־קְשֵׁה־עֹרֶף הוּא וְסָלַחְתָּ לַעֲונֵנוּ וּלְחַטָּאתֵנוּ וּנְחַלְתָּנוּ:
